title: pelargonium hirsutum
intro: Pelargonium hirsutum, also known as the hairy-leaved rose geranium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae. It is native to the western and southern parts of South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of geraniol and other volatile oils. The health benefits of P. hirsutum include reducing inflammation, relieving stress, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues, and it can be prepared into teas, infusions, and essential oils for herbal use.
